insights: Incident assignments are perfectly balanced across all 5 agents, with each agent assigned exactly 100 incidents—no agent bears disproportionate workload or operational strain.

All key categorical dimensions—category, location, priority, and assignment_group—are uniformly distributed, indicating no structural bias or concentration in incident type, geography, urgency, or routing logic.
The absence of missing values and consistent timestamp formatting confirms data integrity, reinforcing that the observed balance is not an artifact of data quality issues.
No evidence of assignment imbalance exists across any measured dimension, eliminating the need for redistribution interventions, workload rebalancing, or agent-level capacity adjustments.
summary: The analysis confirms complete equilibrium in incident assignments: all 5 agents handle identical volumes (100 incidents each), and every categorical dimension—including category, location, priority, and assignment_group—exhibits perfect uniformity. This indicates a well-calibrated assignment system with no operational strain, bottlenecks, or inequity to address. No redistribution or remediation is warranted; ongoing monitoring should focus on preserving this balance rather than correcting non-existent imbalances.
